title: IESET Hypothesis Specification
description: Pre-registered hypothesis with falsification criteria. Every hypothesis in hypotheses/<topic>/<id>.yaml must validate against this schema. First git commit of the file is the pre-registration timestamp for status=pre_registered hypotheses.

version.description: 1 for pre-registered spec. Increment only for post-run alternative specs; never edit v1 after first run.

status.description: Lifecycle state. draft=rough capture from corpus mining, many fields may be placeholders. candidate=human-curated but not yet pre-registered, most fields populated. pre_registered=fully specified, first git commit is the authoritative pre-registration timestamp; no further edits to v1 allowed after first run.

claim.description: One-paragraph plain-English statement of the empirical claim. No hedging weasel-words; hedging lives in prior_confidence and falsification.

claim_direction.description: Optional explicit sign for generic runners when the claim wording is hard to infer reliably.

methodology_note.description: Narrative record of why this hypothesis was respecced: what v1 did, what failed, and what v2+ changes. Required when version > 1 (human-audit signal that the respec is principled, not p-hacked).

evidence_type.description: Causal claims must satisfy METHODOLOGY.md requirements (two-spec rule, natural experiment, or decomposition). Required for candidate and pre_registered. Use canonical_case_multi_metric for single-country canonical cases (Venezuela collapse, Zimbabwe hyperinflation, Great Leap Forward, etc.) where a single-estimator causal test is weaker than a pre-registered pattern-match across many independent extreme outcomes.

mechanism_measurement.description: Structured measurement contract for policy experiments where first-order effects can mask second-order behavioral, supply, allocation, quality, leakage, fiscal, welfare, distributional, institutional, or dynamic effects. Required by project convention for upgraded causal and scoreboard-promoted specs; optional for legacy specs.

intervention_channel.description: Required per invariant 4 if any variable is an intervention measure. Matches the four channels in axes.yaml plus a 'bundled' option for reforms that can't be cleanly separated.

prior_confidence.description: Author's subjective probability hypothesis will be supported, set before run. Required for candidate and pre_registered per invariant 6.

disclosure.description: Brief note on how disclosed priors might bias hypothesis choice, framing, or specification. Required for candidate and pre_registered per invariant 6.

steelman.description: Path to the steelman markdown file. Required for candidate and pre_registered.

scope.description: Canonical scope tags (period, countries, outcome_dim, policy_family, treatment_tags) used by scripts/validate_scope_alignment.py to gate claim→hypothesis links. Added in Phase 2 of the structural scope-alignment fix (April 2026). Authoritative for link validation; may duplicate information in sample.* and variables.outcome but the scope block is the one the validator reads.

covers_claims.description: Explicit reverse-index of which position claims this hypothesis covers, with AUTHORITATIVE polarity. Added Phase 3 (April 2026). When present, this is the SOURCE OF TRUTH for claim-hypothesis linkage: the scoring pipeline reads polarity from here in preference to position.claim_polarity. A reciprocity validator (scripts/validate_link_reciprocity.py) ensures every position claim with linked_hypothesis_id=this hypothesis has a matching entry here, and vice versa. Ownership inversion: positions are no longer the sole source of truth for how their claims relate to hypotheses — the hypothesis declares what it covers, so mis-linked claims surface structurally.

claim_index.description: Zero-based index into the position's falsifiable_specific_claims array.

polarity.description: Authoritative polarity: 'aligned' = position's claim and this hypothesis's claim mean the same thing (a hypothesis SUPPORTED counts as support for the school); 'inverted' = position's claim is the negation (a hypothesis SUPPORTED counts as refutation of the school).

school_prediction.description: Optional mirror of what the school predicts for this hypothesis. Used for reciprocity checks against the position side.

confidence.description: Author confidence in this coverage declaration. 'low' is a signal for Phase 4 manual re-review.

notes.description: Why this claim is covered by this hypothesis. Particularly important for inverted links.

canonical_metrics.description: Pre-registered multi-metric checklist for canonical-case hypotheses. Each metric is an independently-sourced extreme outcome; the hypothesis is supported/refuted based on the count of metrics that meet their thresholds. Required when evidence_type=canonical_case_multi_metric.

metric_id.description: snake_case identifier for this metric within the hypothesis.

threshold.description: Plain-English quantitative threshold, e.g. '>40% decline' or '>15% of population'. Evaluator parses this.

data_quality_flag.description: Optional note on data reliability in the window (e.g. 'Venezuela 2014+ official data censored; use Hanke-Kwok + IMF staff estimates + shadow statistics').

direction.description: Whether meeting this threshold supports or refutes the hypothesis. Defaults to supports_claim.

independence_justification.description: Why this metric is epistemically independent of the others — different data source, different methodology, or different causal layer. Reduces correlated-measurement risk.

support_threshold.description: Minimum metrics-met count to declare support.

refute_threshold.description: Maximum metrics-met count to declare refutation.

source_provenance.description: Set by corpus mining for drafts. Points to anchor(s) in private archive. Not required; not published.

source.description: Canonical form: 'publisher_id:series_identifier'. Composite sources (multiple publishers for different countries) use '; ' as separator, e.g. 'fred:M2SL (USA); ecb:BSI.M.U2 (EU)'. Publisher IDs must be registered in data/fetchers/publishers.yaml.

scope.description: Canonical scope block for claim→hypothesis link gating. Period + countries + outcome_dim are REQUIRED when the scope block is present. policy_family + treatment_tags are optional but strongly recommended for named-policy claims. The validator at scripts/validate_scope_alignment.py compares claim.scope against hypothesis.scope and rejects links with zero overlap on any required axis.

period.description: Start and end year the claim/hypothesis applies to. Inclusive on both ends.

countries.description: ISO3 country list, or one/more group tokens (GLOBAL, OECD, LATAM, EU, EUROZONE, ASIA_EM, AFRICA, MENA, POST_SOVIET, NORDIC, SOUTHERN_EUROPE, ANGLO). Group tokens expand during scope-alignment validation.

outcome_dim.description: Canonical outcome-family tag(s). Controlled vocabulary; extend only via schema PR.

policy_family.description: Broad policy channel(s) the claim/hypothesis intervenes on. Use 'none' for pure-descriptive claims with no policy channel (e.g. 'labour share fell 1980-2020').

treatment_tags.description: Named actors/policies/events specifically tested. Use snake_case canonical tags, e.g. reagan_tax_1981, thatcher_1979, brexit_2016, nafta_1994, tcja_2017, milei_2023, chavismo_1999, volcker_1979, abenomics_2013, agenda_2010, macron_reforms_2017. For generic cross-country panels with no named treatment, leave empty or omit.

mechanism_type.description: Policy mechanism whose second-order channels must be measured separately from the immediate visible effect.

design_features.description: Identification and measurement features that keep second-order effects from being washed out by broad aggregates.

causal_layers.description: Layer-by-layer measurement of direct benefits and second/third-order distortions.

distributional_groups.description: Groups whose effects must be separated before a policy can be treated as distributionally measured.

welfare_accounting.description: Explicit welfare ledger. Use data-gap markers where a component is not yet measured.

promotion_gate.description: Whether the current measurement design can support public scoreboard promotion.

variable_names.description: Names from variables.outcome/treatment/decomposition_channels/controls that operationalise this layer.

candidate_sources.description: Publisher:series or constructed-source candidates, including sources not yet on disk.

required_for_dispositive_verdict.description: True when missing this layer should cap a result at PARTIAL, supported_subset, or inconclusive rather than full SUPPORTED/REFUTED.
